title: Links, Images, References, Autolinks, and URL Safety · Python Build Room
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds reference-aware accessible link and media model from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reference-aware accessible link and media model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset.
Normalize reference labels, detect duplicates, resolve forward references, preserve unresolved source, and report locations.
Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Grammar
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 4555 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Grammar.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse labels, destinations, optional titles, nesting, escaping, angle forms, and balanced delimiters under a named subset. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Grammar, keep Link Grammar connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

This does not satisfy Link Grammar for reference-aware accessible link and media model.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A workshop publishing team handles case 2745 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Link Integrity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Link Integrity record says reference-aware accessible link and media model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the proposed evidence packet evidence boundary for Link Integrity.

For audit the proposed evidence packet, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Link Integrity for reference-aware accessible link and media model.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-cc3746" that uses case 1575 to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Link Integrity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Link Integrity into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Link Integrity, keep Link Integrity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-link-integrity-cc3746
# Competency: ssg-link-integrity.
# Case 1575: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_link_integrity_cc3746(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_cc3746 = "cc3746-6473cc-1575"
    assert evidence_variant_cc3746
    evidence_axes_cc3746 = (
        "readercc3746",
        "source1575",
        "parse6473cc",
        "routecc37461575",
        "artifact1575cc3746",
        "recovery6473cc1575",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_cc3746)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"
